other_observers: Heather Morris, Leland Flores (initially found bird), Amy Dobbins, Jan Rees, Kristi Streiffert; Leland Flores
specific_location: 141 E. 16th St., Durango, CO (Durango Fish Hatchery)
description: This bird is American Robin-size with a blue-gray back and orange underparts. The bill is dark and slightly longer than an American Robin robin with a slight downward curve. The bird has a thick, dark "collar" across the top of the breast. The bird has a dark face with an orange supercilium. The upper tail is also blue gray. The throat of the bird is orange. There are two orange wing bars.Behaviors: This bird has been observed mainly when it flies down to the ground to pick and scratch underneath large spruce trees. It is extremely shy and return into the thick, inner branches of the spruces at the slightest disturbance. Recently it has started eating dried mealworms that we have put out for it under a spruce in our yard. It quickly runs from worm to worm and then usually goes to drink from a small, permanent puddle in the yard. It was observed being aggressive toward a nearby Dark-eyed Junco once, but normally pays the birds feeding around it no attention.Call: We have not heard any vocalizations from the bird.Plumage: Adult
